name: Senomorphics bootstrap for June 1, 2026
summary: First public baseline bundle for senomorphics under cellular senescence, centered on small human biomarker signals and mechanistic SASP-modulation evidence.

scope.question: What is the current human-relevant evidence landscape for Senomorphics, and what belongs in the first public baseline?

proposed_changes.summary: Add topical rapamycin human skin senescence source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source anchors the most direct human senescence-marker signal for rapamycin-class senomorphic evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add metformin older-adult muscle senescence/SASP source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source supplies a direct human skeletal-muscle senescence/SASP biomarker signal for metformin in an acute stress-recovery model.

proposed_changes.summary: Add JAK inhibition SASP and aged-mouse frailty source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source anchors the main preclinical functional senomorphic signal and human-cell SASP suppression evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add metformin IKK/NF-kB SASP mechanism source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source explains metformin's senomorphic mechanism without upgrading it beyond mechanistic evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add mTOR IL1A SASP mechanism source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source supports rapamycin-class SASP suppression through an mTOR-linked IL1A mechanism.

proposed_changes.summary: Add mTOR MAPKAPK2 SASP mechanism source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source provides a second rapamycin-sensitive SASP-control mechanism through MAPKAPK2/ZFP36L1.

proposed_changes.summary: Add oral rapamycin older-adult feasibility source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source constrains systemic rapamycin interpretation by separating short-term feasibility from functional efficacy.

proposed_changes.summary: Add topical rapamycin human skin study.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record links the human p16 skin biomarker signal to the senomorphics track.

proposed_changes.summary: Add metformin older-adult disuse/recovery study.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record links older-adult muscle senescence/SASP markers to a bounded metformin stress-recovery context.

proposed_changes.summary: Add JAK inhibition SASP and aged-mouse frailty study.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record captures preclinical function plus human-cell SASP suppression without treating it as human efficacy.

proposed_changes.summary: Add metformin SASP mechanism study.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record keeps metformin's IKK/NF-kB-linked SASP mechanism distinct from human outcome claims.

proposed_changes.summary: Add mTOR IL1A senescent-cell SASP study.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record links rapamycin-sensitive mTOR/IL1A biology to SASP suppression.

proposed_changes.summary: Add mTOR MAPKAPK2 senescent-cell SASP study.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record supports rapamycin-class SASP suppression while preserving the in-vitro boundary.

proposed_changes.summary: Add oral rapamycin older-adult feasibility study.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record supplies a systemic human rapamycin safety and no-functional-change boundary.

proposed_changes.summary: Add topical rapamycin human p16 biomarker finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding anchors the track's human biomarker stage assignment.

proposed_changes.summary: Add metformin older-adult muscle senescence/SASP finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding adds a direct but context-limited human senescence/SASP biomarker signal.

proposed_changes.summary: Add JAK inhibition preclinical function finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supplies the strongest preclinical functional bridge for senomorphic SASP modulation.

proposed_changes.summary: Add metformin IKK/NF-kB SASP mechanism finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports senomorphic plausibility while remaining mechanistic.

proposed_changes.summary: Add mTOR IL1A SASP mechanism finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports rapamycin-class SASP modulation through IL1A translation control.

proposed_changes.summary: Add mTOR MAPKAPK2 SASP mechanism finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding adds a second mTOR-SASP mechanism for rapamycin-class senomorphic plausibility.

proposed_changes.summary: Add oral rapamycin functional-boundary finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding prevents systemic rapamycin feasibility data from being overread as functional senomorphic efficacy.

proposed_changes.summary: Update metformin intervention cross-links for senomorphics.
proposed_changes.rationale: Metformin now has staged senomorphic studies and findings, so the normalized intervention record should expose the cross-track link while preserving AMPK attribution limits.

proposed_changes.summary: Update sirolimus intervention cross-links for senomorphics.
proposed_changes.rationale: Rapamycin-class evidence is central to this baseline, so sirolimus should link to senomorphic staged studies and findings.

proposed_changes.summary: Add first public senomorphics track outlook.
proposed_changes.rationale: The staged source and finding set supports a conservative low-confidence human_biomarker_signal baseline with explicit functional and safety boundaries.
uncertainty_flags: Human evidence is small and context-specific
SASP suppression has context-dependent safety and repair tradeoffs

proposed_outlook_implications.note: A first public baseline is justified at low-confidence human_biomarker_signal because topical rapamycin and metformin have direct but small human senescence/SASP biomarker signals, while mechanistic and aged-mouse data support SASP modulation without establishing durable human function.
Publishing this bundle would broaden cellular senescence coverage beyond senolytics by adding a phenotype-modulation track with clear boundaries from cell clearance.

next_actions: Publication complete. Review downstream pages if this change affects shared surfaces.
